repositories.brief_summary: Major week with the typing recovery mechanism landing, Seq.ints_in_range added to stdlib, and significant runtime and documentation work.
repositories.new_features_summary: Typing recovery for multi-error reporting, Seq.ints_in_range, and a -typing-recovery compiler flag were the headline additions.
repositories.new_features:
- **Typing recovery** ([#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)): Merlin's typing recovery mechanism is now upstream, enabled via `-typing-recovery`; when active the typechecker no longer stops at the first error but continues and collects all errors in a file — a major quality-of-life improvement for editor integration
- **`Seq.ints_in_range`** ([#10480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10480)): New `Seq.ints_in_range ~first ~last` function added to the standard library, producing a bounded integer sequence (inclusive on both ends); annotated `@since 5.6`
- **`-typing-recovery` compiler flag** ([#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)): New compiler CLI flag wired through `ocamlc` and `ocamlopt` to enable the multi-error typing mode
- **Fix for `caml_ba_alloc` SUBARRAY crash** ([#14820](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14820)): Backward-compat fix so that old C bindings passing `CAML_BA_SUBARRAY` with `data = NULL` no longer result in an OOM crash; cherry-picked to the 5.5 branch ([#14825](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14825))
- **Nicer PDF manual fonts** ([#14819](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14819)): PDF manual now uses Utopia and Cabin fonts (sans-serif for chapter and section titles), replacing the previous default LaTeX fonts
- **Documentation**: `Format` module documentation updated with a working example ([#14826](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14826)); `ocamlrun` manpage synchronised with the manual for `OCAMLRUNPARAM` options ([#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14817)); removed stale long-lost `ocamlrun` option from its manpage ([#14815](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14815))
repositories.activity_summary: 15 PRs merged across stdlib, runtime, typechecker, and documentation; 24 open PRs active including a new GC-pacing logic proposal and several ephemeron correctness fixes.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened** [#14821](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14821) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole): disable "cold" tests in GitHub Actions CI to speed up the mandatory CI loop
- **Newly opened** [#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): follow-up to typing recovery — freeze paths in the modular-explicits interaction
- **Newly opened** [#14822](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14822) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): extended testsuite for sensible and edge-case `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing
- **Newly opened** [#14818](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14818) by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok): add a testsuite test that fragments sizeclass pools
- **Newly opened** [#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14814) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): add `caml_result_find_exception` to `mlvalues.h`
- **Newly opened** [#14812](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14812) by [@agrn](https://github.com/agrn): `comballoc` — emit a single add after a combined allocation
- **Newly opened** [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): ensure no orphaned ephemerons remain across GC cycles
- **Typechecker**: [#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241) merged — large typing recovery infrastructure (new `typing_recovery.ml`, `typing_recovery_state.ml`, extensive test suite with ~80 new test cases); [#14776](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14776) and [#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14789) fixed `moregen`/GADT and scope-escape corner cases; [#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754) fixed cyclic types introduced by `with type` module constraints; [#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14051) improved error messages for first-class modules with missing constrained types
- **Runtime**: [#14816](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14816) fixed a data race when marking lazy objects using `cmpxchg`; [#14699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14699) removed unused `caml_stat_alloc_aligned[_noexc]` functions
- **Build and docs**: [#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) minor build fixes for cross-compilation; [#14759](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14759) exposed `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing functions for testing
- **Still open / in progress**: [#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) new GC-pacing logic by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez); ephemeron orphaning cluster ([#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche); extensible IO channels RFC implementation [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube); constructor eta-expansion proposal [#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch)
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion around ephemeron correctness in the GC, the new GC-pacing logic proposal, and the typing recovery follow-up for modular explicits.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Ephemeron correctness cluster**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has multiple open PRs ([#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813)) addressing ephemeron orphaning across GC cycles; these interact with the Changes sync entries noting `#13629` major GC ephemeron cleanup
- **New GC-pacing logic** [#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796): [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) opened a substantial PR implementing a new GC-pacing algorithm (from https://github.com/damiendoligez/gc-pacing), listed as the last item for issue [#14324](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14324); still has open test failures to debug
- **Typing recovery follow-ups**: immediately after landing [#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824) to address a remaining interaction with modular-explicits path freezing
- **Constructor eta-expansion** [#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803): [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) proposed automatically eta-expanding argument-less constructors in function position (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`) without new syntax — marked as preliminary/for-discussion
repositories.trends_summary: Convergence toward OCaml 5.6 features: typing recovery upstreamed from Merlin, stdlib growth, and continued GC correctness hardening for multicore.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Merlin-to-compiler upstreaming**: the landing of typing recovery ([#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)) continues the trend of absorbing Merlin's compiler patches upstream, reducing the maintenance burden for the Merlin team
- **GC correctness and pacing**: significant attention on ephemeron correctness ([#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734)) and a new GC-pacing proposal ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) indicate active work to improve runtime reliability and predictability for multicore OCaml
- **Stdlib surface area expanding**: `Seq.ints_in_range` joins recent additions like `List.append_map`, `Array.fold_{left,right}2`, `Fun.todo` — steady incremental growth of the standard library
- **Documentation quality push**: PDF manual font improvements, manpage synchronization, and `Format` documentation updates reflect ongoing effort to improve user-facing documentation quality

repositories.brief_summary: Two API refactoring PRs merged advancing the repository performance overhaul, with several new PRs opened for reftests and new .install file sections.
repositories.new_features_summary: New OpamPatch and OpamRepositoryRoot modules landed, abstracting patch and repository root operations for cleaner API design.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`OpamPatch` module** extracted from `OpamSystem`, providing dedicated `translate_patch`, `patch`, and `parse_patch` functions — part of the API cleanup enabling the larger repository performance overhaul ([#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934))
- New **`OpamRepositoryRoot` module** in `opam-repository` abstracts over repository roots (currently backed by a `Dir` implementation), offering quarantine, backup, move, copy, patch, and path operations behind a clean interface ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680))
- **`OpamRepositoryPath` functorized** via a new `Make` functor, allowing repository path construction to be parameterized over different filepath separator implementations — required for cross-platform correctness ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680))
- Updated `master_changes.md` changelog entries documenting the `OpamPatch` creation and `OpamRepositoryRoot` addition for downstream library consumers ([#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934), [#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680))
repositories.activity_summary: Several new PRs opened covering .install file extensions, opam config subst fixes, reftests for repository handling, and tar library integration.
repositories.activity:
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened three PRs advancing the repository overhaul story ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)): HTTP repository reftests ([#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939)), a comprehensive repository layout reftest that logs load-from-dir vs load-from-diff paths ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941)), and a `tar` library integration replacing the `tar` system tool for archive creation ([#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)); a draft extraction of `OpamAdminRepoUpgrade` changes was also opened ([#6942](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6942))
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) opened a PR adding **`prefix` and `prefixexec` sections** to `.install` files, which would allow packages to install files relative to a custom prefix ([#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)), addressing feature request [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919)
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) opened a fix for **`opam config subst` absolute path handling** ([#6936](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6936)) resolving [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925), and also has an ongoing PR offering to **remove auto-pinned packages on install failure** ([#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923)) queued on [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) has an open documentation PR **homogenising man page highlighting** of options, arguments, and environment variables to use bold `$(b,...)` consistently ([#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921)), resolving [#6112](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6112)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s large WIP **repository performance overhaul** ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) continues to progress as prerequisite PRs land — the `OpamPatch` extraction ([#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934)) and `OpamRepositoryRoot` abstraction ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)) both merged this week
repositories.discussions_summary: Active issue reporting around opam lock correctness, a Windows path separator bug, and a patch application failure in opam update.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported that `opam update` fails with `OpamSystem.Internal_patch_error` for certain git repository transitions, narrowed the root cause to the OCaml `opatch` library mishandling patches where a file is modified and then copied ([#6937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6937)), and filed an upstream report at [hannesm/patch#43](https://github.com/hannesm/patch/issues/43)
- [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) filed two related `opam lock` correctness issues: undefined boolean filter variables (like `with-dev-setup`) becoming fatal under `--locked` instead of defaulting to false ([#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946)), and `opam lock` incorrectly reporting a constraint as unsatisfied despite the package being installed in the current switch ([#6944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6944))
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) offered structural review feedback on the repository layout reftest ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941)), suggesting organizing test cases per layout (showing all commands for each layout) rather than per command, to reduce output size and improve readability
- [@yannl35133](https://github.com/yannl35133) reported a misleading error when adding a repository that already exists globally with a different URL, and proposed clearer wording that explains the global nature of the mapping and the distinction between `add` and `set-url` ([#6943](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6943))
- [Nicolas Berthier](https://github.com/nberth) reported that `opam install dir/*.opam` fails on Windows with an "Invalid character '/' in package name" error despite Windows supporting `/` as a path separator ([#6940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6940))
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained build-up of API abstractions and test infrastructure as prerequisites for the large repository performance overhaul.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **repository performance overhaul** ([#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) is advancing through a deliberate queued-PR strategy: each week sees one or two prerequisite abstractions merged (`OpamPatch` ([#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934)), `OpamRepositoryRoot` ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680))), followed immediately by the next dependency (`OpamTar` ([#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945))) being opened and queued
- **Repository handling test coverage** is growing deliberately alongside the refactoring work, with both HTTP fetch/update reftests ([#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939)) and comprehensive layout reftests ([#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941)) opened this week to lock in current behaviour before the `tar.gz`-based rewrite lands
- **`opam lock` correctness** is drawing attention from users, with two distinct locking bugs filed in the same week ([#6944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6944), [#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946)), suggesting the feature may have edge cases worth a focused audit

- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) opened a PR adding `prefix` and `prefixexec` sections to `.install` files ([#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)) and participated in a discussion about `bwrap`-related test environment issues
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) opened a documentation consistency PR for man page highlighting ([#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921))
- [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) reported two `opam lock` correctness bugs ([#6944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6944), [#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946))

repositories.brief_summary: FreeBSD portability discussion continued and automated Nix flake lock update bumped merlin and nixpkgs.
repositories.activity_summary: Automated Nix flake lock update open; ongoing discussion on FreeBSD build failures linked to vendored lev.
repositories.activity:
- The automated **Nix flake lock update** PR ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) remains open, bumping `merlin5_3` to commit `77009b69` (2026-05-22) and `nixpkgs` to the nix-overlays snapshot from 2026-05-23, submitted by the `github-actions` bot.
- Discussion on the closed **FreeBSD 15.0 build failure** issue ([#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)) continued into this week, with [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) raising the option of applying the upstream `lev` portability fix directly in the vendored copy, and [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) advising that any local patch should also be validated on FreeBSD `stable/14`.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on FreeBSD build failure explores patching the vendored lev library as a temporary workaround.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) continued discussing the **FreeBSD portability failure** in ([#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)), which traces back to `rgrinberg/lev` hard-coding `<sys/statfs.h>` — a Linux-specific header. [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) proposed patching the vendored copy of `lev` directly rather than waiting for an upstream release, and [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) (a FreeBSD user affected by the bug) offered to help test on `stable/14` if needed.
repositories.trends_summary: Recurring FreeBSD compatibility issues with the vendored lev library point to a need for an upstream fix or local patch.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The **FreeBSD build failure** has resurfaced multiple times ([#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605), previously [#1459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1459), [#747](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/747), [#1069](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1069)), all rooted in the vendored `lev` library's use of Linux-specific system headers. The discussion this week suggests the project is moving toward applying a local patch in the vendored copy rather than waiting for an infrequent upstream release.
repositories.contributors_summary: Zaraksh Rahman reported and investigated the FreeBSD build issue; Ulysse reviewed and engaged on portability fix options.
repositories.contributors:
- [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) reported the **FreeBSD 15.0 build failure** ([#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)), investigated its root cause in the vendored `lev` library, opened a fix upstream, and provided guidance on testing across FreeBSD versions.
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed the situation on ([#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)) and proposed applying the portability fix directly in the vendored `lev` copy as a pragmatic short-term solution.

repositories.brief_summary: Three PRs merged covering maintenance fixes; two major feature PRs (LRgrep integration and ocamlgrep) and several bug-fix PRs remain open under review.
repositories.new_features_summary: Two significant feature PRs opened: LRgrep-based declarative syntax error messages and structural search of OCaml code via ocamlgrep.
repositories.new_features:
- [#2072](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2072) **Integrate LRgrep error messages** by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def): adds a declarative `parse_errors.lrgrep` specification and vendor-copied LRgrep runtime to generate better, more consistent syntax error messages; includes a `make lrgrep-coverage` target to report grammar constructs lacking error coverage. (+11900/-6714 lines across 27 files, opened 2026-05-23)
- [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064) **Add ocamlgrep: structural search of OCaml code** by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon): tentative integration of the ocamlgrep project adding a new `ocamlgrep` subcommand that uses `dune describe workspace` to find `.cmt` files and an `Expr_search` module to match typed expressions with optional type annotations and wildcards. (+1792/-25 lines, opened 2026-05-06, actively reviewed this week)
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs were merged and five PRs remain open, covering parser improvements, bug fixes, and feature additions.
repositories.activity:
- [#2072](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2072) **Integrate LRgrep error messages** by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def) — open, under review; major addition of declarative syntax error infrastructure

repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on ocamlgrep focused on dependency hygiene, reuse of existing Merlin internals, and error reporting strategy.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064) ocamlgrep review: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) flagged that depending on `base` is not acceptable for Merlin and suggested reusing `Cmt_cache` and `Locate` module functionality rather than reimplementing them; [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) removed the `ppx_sexp_conv` dependency in favour of a hand-rolled `csexp`-based implementation and added an `errors` field to the JSON response to surface user-facing parse errors
- [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2066) locate fix for let punnings: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted the fix improves `locate` but makes `occurrences` behaviour less intuitive since both queries now resolve to the expression side; the trade-off is acknowledged as acceptable but a more robust fix remains desirable
- [Issue #1708](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1708): new comment from [@arvidj](https://github.com/arvidj) noting support for toplevel directives (`#use`, `#require`) existed in Merlin 3.1.0 and expressing interest in restoring it
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows a push toward richer error reporting infrastructure and structural code search, alongside ongoing memory and correctness improvements.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Declarative error message infrastructure**: the LRgrep PR ([#2072](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2072)) introduces a pattern-based approach to specifying syntax error messages, with built-in coverage tooling, signalling a longer-term effort to replace ad-hoc error recovery logic
- **Structural code search**: the ocamlgrep PR ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064)) continues to evolve toward integration as a first-class Merlin subcommand, with review feedback nudging the implementation to reuse existing Merlin infrastructure
- **Memory management for the indexer**: the LRU PR ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2050)) addresses unbounded memory growth in the index cache, reflecting ongoing attention to resource usage at scale

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with scheduler/file-watcher refactoring, Melange single-context work, and the 3.23.1 release landing on main.
repositories.new_features_summary: New `%{bin:...}` bin-layout support, directory targets enabled in Dune 3.24, and Melange-specific preprocess fields added.
repositories.new_features:
- **`%{bin:...}` bin-layout**: [#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432) introduces a bin-layout mode for `%{bin:...}` path-form dependencies, allowing executables to be referenced via their installed layout path.
- **Directory targets available in Dune 3.24**: [#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579) makes directory targets a first-class feature gated on `(lang dune 3.24)`.
- **Melange-specific preprocess fields**: [#14572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14572) adds `melange.preprocess` and `melange.preprocessor_deps` stanza fields, gated on Dune 3.24, for Melange-only preprocessing configuration.
- **Melange single-context: conditional preprocessor deps**: [#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14681) and [#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14680) track compilation contexts by mode and add conditional Melange preprocessor dependencies as part of the single-context initiative.
- **Typed `Filename.t`**: [#14545](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14545) makes `Filename.t` an abstract type with invariants (non-empty, no `/`, not `.` or `..`), adding construction and conversion functions.
- **`rev_store` cache versioning**: [#14494](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14494) enables and versions the `rev_store` cache used by package management.
- **RPC long-poll cancellation**: [#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14629) adds the ability to cancel long-poll RPC requests without responding, with supporting tests in [#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14622), [#14623](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14623), [#14625](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14625), [#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14626), [#14627](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14627).
- **Fiber map-reduce helpers**: [#14503](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14503) adds `map-reduce` combinators to the `Fiber` library.
- **Profile name validation**: [#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14657) validates profile names in the `env` stanza, fixing [#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14630).
repositories.activity_summary: Over 90 PRs were active this week, with newly opened work on per-module library filtering, Melange single-context installation, and file watcher improvements.
repositories.activity:
- [#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14694) (open): `fs_memo` should avoid recording watches in batch mode, preventing stale watches from leaking into watch-mode builds.
- [#14702](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14702) (open): Fix parent directory watches for external files — `parent_directory` was returning the original path instead of the discovered directory.
- [#14705](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14705) (open): Trace file watcher events in the scheduler for all backends, not just inotify.
- [#14707](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14707) (open): Fix thread pool worker spawning when queued work exceeds idle capacity.
- [#14677](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14677) (open): Track allocation sources in allocation traces, grouping by Memprof source and callstack.
- [#14672](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14672) (open, draft): Melange single-context installation support.
- [#14636](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14636) (open): Update vendored cmdliner to 2.1.1, fixing [#12515](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12515).
- [#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14621) (open, draft): 3.24.0 release branch.
- [#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14682) merged: Melange single-context — add `melange.libraries` field.
- [#14664](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14664) merged: `dune show targets` now accepts build-only directories, fixing [#13782](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13782).
- [#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14668) merged: Clearer error when `%{target}` is used without a `(target ...)` field, fixing [#12439](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12439).
- [#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14703) merged: Fix thread pool accounting on task failure.
- [#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14538) merged: Switch revdeps CI from Nix-based to opam-based tests, resolving [#13897](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13897).
- [#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544) merged: Add `oxcaml-compiler` to the list of recognized compiler packages.
- [#14499](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14499) merged: Reject `(package ...)` inside named dependency bindings.
- [#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14710) merged: Revert of [#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14665) — the `dune show targets` directory-listing feature was reverted after second thoughts.
- [#14674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14674) merged: Send file watcher events directly to the scheduler, removing the intermediate thunk queue.
- [#14678](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14678) merged: Fix Windows watcher sync handling — sync directory was not created on setup.
repositories.discussions_summary: Ongoing debate around `dune show targets` design and directory listing, and per-module library narrowing for build correctness.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The `dune show targets` directory-listing feature ([#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14665)) was merged and then reverted ([#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14710)) after [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) reconsidered the design. Related draft PRs [#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14666) and [#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14667) for directory traversal and hiding source files were also closed pending further design discussion referencing [#8167](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/8167).
- The 3.24.0 release tracker [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620) is open with the release branch [#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14621) created; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) noted that [#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544) (OxCaml compiler support) should be included before cutting the release.
- The 9-layer per-module library dependency filtering stack ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14492) by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)) saw active review from [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), with discussions on whether Melange compilation contexts could also benefit from the narrowing.
- The tool management design draft [#13457](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13457) received multiple comments from [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) raising questions about multi-version tooling UX and alignment with planned auto-locking work.
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained effort on scheduler decoupling, file watcher correctness across platforms, and the Melange single-context work advancing steadily.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Scheduler and file watcher refactoring**: A sustained series of PRs from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) this week ([#14669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14669), [#14670](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14670), [#14671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14671), [#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14673), [#14674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14674), [#14675](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14675), [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14676)) systematically reduces coupling between the scheduler, file watcher backends, and the build loop.
- **Melange single-context mode**: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) is advancing Melange support under a unified build context with multiple PRs this week ([#14572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14572), [#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14680), [#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14681), [#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14682)) preparing infrastructure for the `single-context-universal` label.
- **Per-module dependency narrowing**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)'s 9-layer PR stack ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14492)) aims to reduce unnecessary recompilation by filtering library deps and include flags per module rather than per compilation context.
- **Command.run API consolidation**: Multiple PRs from [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([#14647](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14647)–[#14656](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14656)) consistently adopt `Command.run ~sandbox` and `~env` parameters across all rule-generating modules.
repositories.issues_summary: Several long-standing issues were closed this week including a confusing `%{target}` error message and two `dune show targets` limitations.
repositories.good_first_issues:

- [#11407](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11407) (open): Make error messages for unknown extension versions more useful by listing available versions when a `(using ...)` stanza references an unsupported version.

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on whether Findlib should install .cmx files to suppress warning 58 in downstream projects.

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml kind annotations support merged, mdx test infrastructure removed, and OxCaml rendering work continues.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml kind annotations now rendered in HTML, LaTeX, man, and markdown outputs via new Kind.t model type.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml kind annotations** are now parsed and rendered across all output formats (HTML, LaTeX, man, markdown), introducing a new `Kind.t` model type with variants for `Default`, `Abbreviation`, `Mod`, `With`, `Kind_of`, and `Product` — kind information is propagated through type parameters and type declarations, with annotations displayed inline in generated docs by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410))
- **OxCaml implementation file rendering** is now tested, covering how `.ml` files without an interface are rendered by odoc using a sample drawn directly from OxCaml documentation, by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) ([#1424](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1424))
- **Poly type variable kind annotations** now carry jkind information in both `.cmi` and `.cmti` loaders: `Ttyp_poly` vars in cmti now use `Cmi.read_jkind_annotation` rather than ignoring layout data ([963b942](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/commit/963b942657668d490bcdbb571611e9dcec8485b2))
repositories.activity_summary: Three new OxCaml-related PRs remain open while mdx tests were removed and the OxCaml unboxed types issue was closed.
repositories.activity:
- **Three open PRs awaiting review**: modalities support by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) which builds on the newly merged kind annotations work ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), zero alloc annotation support by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) with active discussion on component type propagation ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), and a fix for multiline links in comments by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) ([#1439](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1439))
- **mdx test infrastructure removed** — [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) deleted ~10,500 lines of mdx tests from `src/xref2` (test.md, refs.md, resolve/test.md, subst/test.md, strengthen/test.md, cpath/cpath_test.md) and removed mdx from CI workflows, following agreement from [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) that cram tests provide better coverage ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437))
- **OxCaml unboxed types issue closed** — [#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414) tracking missing `#` rendering for unboxed named types was closed, resolved by the earlier PR [#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)
- **AI-generated empty PR closed** — [#1438](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1438) auto-generated by an LLM (LM Studio) to fix [#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846) was closed as empty, with [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) noting the "LGTM" was sarcasm about the emptiness
repositories.discussions_summary: Active review on zero alloc annotation propagation through component types, and agreement to remove legacy mdx tests.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **zero alloc annotation PR** [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) generated detailed discussion between [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) on how to correctly propagate zero_alloc info through odoc's intermediate component types — [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) noted that without adding `extr_attrs` to `Value.t` in `xref2/components.ml`, the attribute would be silently dropped in wrapped libraries
- **mdx test removal** was discussed in [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) where [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) first proposed removal, [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) agreed they had never caught a bug not covered by cram tests, and [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) then opened [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437) to carry out the cleanup
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml feature support is being built incrementally through a coordinated set of PRs tracked in issue #1411.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **OxCaml support effort** is advancing systematically through a tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411), with kind annotations ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)) and unboxed types ([#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)) now resolved, and modalities ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and zero alloc annotations ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) actively in review
- **Test infrastructure modernization** — the removal of mdx-based tests ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437)) in favor of cram tests reflects a longer-running shift in how the project validates cross-reference and resolution behavior
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors were active this week across code authorship, merges, and code review.
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored and had merged the OxCaml kind annotations implementation ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)) and the mdx test removal ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437)), and has the OxCaml modalities PR open awaiting review ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420))
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) had the OxCaml implementation file test merged ([#1424](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1424)) and is actively developing zero alloc annotation support ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), incorporating review feedback on component type propagation

repositories.brief_summary: TLS security fixes (2 CVEs), dune 3.23.1, lrgrep 0.9, Windows CI infrastructure overhaul, and many new package submissions.
repositories.new_features_summary: TLS 2.1.0 patches two CVEs; lrgrep 0.9 debuts; OCaml 5.4.1+lrgrep fork improves error messages; valkey adds typed Bundle wrappers.
repositories.new_features:
- **tls 2.1.0** fixes two security vulnerabilities: `keyUsage`/`extendedKeyUsage` validation was missing from the server (CVE-2026-45389 / OSEC-2026-07) and from TLS 1.3 client handshakes (CVE-2026-45388 / OSEC-2026-06), reported by Ben Smyth; also adds `drain_handshake` improvements for half-closed connections ([#29930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29930))
- **lrgrep 0.9** first publicly announced release — provides precise enumeration/coverage, JSON report output, a "Getting started" guide, and a `lrgrep.top` functor for customising lexical syntax; applied to OCaml with exhaustive coverage ([#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928))
- **ocaml-compiler 5.4.1+lrgrep** experimental compiler fork by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) integrates lrgrep-based improved syntax error messages into OCaml 5.4.1; tagged `avoid-version` so it is only selected when explicitly requested ([#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932))
- **valkey 0.4.0** adds typed OCaml wrappers for Valkey Bundle Search (`Valkey.Search`), JSON (`Valkey.Json`), and Bloom filters (`Valkey.Bloom`), along with runnable examples ([#29916](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29916))
- **tar 3.5.0** fixes a security issue — integer overflows from `Int64.to_int` on 32-bit platforms — in tar-unix, tar-lwt-unix, and tar-eio; raises OCaml lower bound to 4.13.0 ([#29940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29940))
- **dune 3.23.1** fixes menhir opam dependency injection (no longer adds `menhir` to packages that didn't declare it), restores 3.22 behaviour for dune version-bound deduplication on older `lang dune` versions, fixes fallback to the secondary compiler for packages with OCaml upper bounds below 4.14, and fixes the bootstrap on NetBSD ([#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29903))
- **awa 0.6.0** (SSH in OCaml) adds a server `eof` function and replaces `cstruct.t` with `string`/`Buffer.t` for internal state ([#29948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29948))
- **out-channel-redirect 0.2** adds `capture_channels`, `capture_channels_interleaved`, `capture_outputs`, and `capture_outputs_interleaved` for capturing lists of channels; `capture` is now an alias for `capture_outputs_interleaved` ([#29922](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29922))
- **odep 0.2.2** adds `--with-modules` argument to dune dependency graphs, fixes opam 2.4 compatibility, and fixes vertex name conflicts when a dune library and executable share a name ([#29935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29935))
- **Windows dependency graph overhaul** by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): fixes incorrect `{post}` dependencies in `ocaml-env-*` packages that allowed uninstalling host-arch/host-system packages to break compiler invariants; moves `mingw-w64-shims` into `ocaml-env-mingw` where it belongs; introduces `x-revision` fields for tracking in-place package updates ([#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907))
- **conf-rocksdb 2** migrated to use `pkg-config` for detection, with distro-specific package names for Alpine and CentOS added ([#29919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29919))
- **conf-bzip2.1** refactored to correctly distinguish platforms that cannot use `pkg-config` for bzip2 detection (FreeBSD, older Ubuntu/Debian, Cygwin) from those that should use `pkgconf` (NetBSD, MSYS2) ([#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29912))
- **symphony-orchestrator-tui 0.1.0** new terminal UI toolkit for OCaml with a buffered renderer, Toffee-backed flexbox layout, reusable widgets, key parsing, keymaps, and viewport helpers ([#29910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29910))
- **ecma-regex 0.1.0** new library for ECMAScript regular-expression syntax and matching semantics, including Unicode property escapes, raw UTF-16 ECMAScript String APIs, and JSON Schema regex compatibility ([#29915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29915))
- **ocaml-dockerfile 8.4.0** adds `Distro.gcc_version` function for querying GCC versions per distro ([#29927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29927))

repositories.discussions_summary: Windows dependency management policy, ocaml-solo5 constraint semantics, and Z3 GCC version compatibility were the main discussion threads.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Windows compiler dependency invariant** discussion in [#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) debated whether conf packages should use `x-revision` fields or version bumps to track in-place fixes; consensus leaned toward `x-revision` to avoid leaving known-broken old packages accessible to users
- **ocaml-solo5 cross-compiler invariant** in [#29944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29944): [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) explained that the current `=` version constraint makes it easy to reason about host/cross compiler ABI compatibility; relaxing it without a clear invariant could silently break mirage cross-compilation setups
- **Z3 4.16.0 GCC version handling** in [#29933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29933): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed adding multiple older distros (centos-7, centos-9, debian-12, ubuntu-22.04, opensuse-15.6, oraclelinux-7) to `x-ci-accept-failures` due to C++20 `<format>` header unavailability on older GCC versions, acknowledging the limitation
- **conf-bzip2 versioning policy** in [#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29912): [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) questioned whether large conf package changes should require a version bump; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) argued that editing in-place is less imperfect than leaving permanent bugs in old versions, with [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) acknowledging both perspectives
- **Cram test strategy** discussion in [#29929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29929): [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) advised [Emile Trotignon](https://github.com/EmileTrotignon) that including cram tests in opam releases is fragile since unrelated dep updates can break them for trivial formatting reasons, and suggested focusing on tests that specifically validate important behaviour
repositories.trends_summary: Windows CI robustness, MirageOS metrics deduplication, and security-motivated constraint tightening are recurring themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows CI infrastructure maturation**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) continues systematic overhaul of Windows packaging, having now addressed the host-arch/host-system dependency bug, `mingw-w64-shims` placement, and `conf-bzip2` detection logic across several consecutive PRs ([#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907), [#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29912)); the introduction of `x-revision` fields signals a policy shift toward tracked in-place updates rather than unbounded version proliferation
- **MirageOS metrics deduplication pattern**: Multiple MirageOS packages (mirage-net-solo5, utcp) received the same fix — removing per-instance `Metrics.Src.t` and relying on the `ifname`/`stack-id` tag instead — suggesting a coordinated cleanup effort ([#29946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29946), [#29947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29947))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility wave**: Several packages and constraint updates this week mention OCaml 5.5 (camlp5, `lwt.5.10.0` upper bound, the lrgrep compiler fork); the ecosystem is actively preparing for an upcoming 5.5 release
- **LR-based error message tooling gaining traction**: The simultaneous landing of lrgrep 0.9 ([#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928)) and the `ocaml-compiler.5.4.1+lrgrep` fork ([#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932)) shows a coordinated effort to bring improved parser error messages to the OCaml ecosystem, with the long-term goal of merging into a future compiler release
- **AI-adjacent OCaml packages increasing**: `openrouter_api` ([#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29914)) — a unified LLM gateway client — and the AI-assisted `MlFront_ProgressZig` (noted in [#29918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29918) crosslink) reflect a growing interest in AI tooling within the OCaml ecosystem

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update merged with precise SIMD semantics; Wasm exception handling fixes; 6.4 release tracking opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml stubs and SIMD fixes merged; Wasm closure type naming and exception handler traversal corrected.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update** merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), adding precise SIMD `float32` min/max bytecode semantics (matching x86 `MINSS`/`MAXSS` instruction behavior), new stubs for `caml_no_bytecode_impl` and `caml_reinterpret_unboxed_int64_as_tagged_int63`, renamed `caml_float16_to_double` to `caml_double_of_float16`, and improved `caml_obj_dup` ([#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225))
- **Wasm: non-ambiguous closure env type names** fix resolves a struct type clash where `no_code_pointer`-optimised closures and genuine arity-0 closures registered the same type name with incompatible bodies, causing `struct.new` inconsistencies in loop hoisting and WASI builds ([#2246](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2246))
- **Wasm: `needed_handlers` traversal** corrected to walk the dominator subtree rather than CFG-reachable blocks, preventing dead handler blocks from being emitted inside exception wrappers when `Poptrap` edges escaped the structural region ([#2244](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2244))
- **Wasm runtime small fixes** including bound check bug in `marshal` and incorrect `O_APPEND` mask in `caml_sys_open`, with new regression tests added for both ([#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240), [57f5229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/57f5229956fd8e30ebb2937ff205006d5c3ef04e))
- **Menhir lower bound** declared in `dune-project` and opam files ([a09db97](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/a09db97bae6da86177d8a072e2280807c40b36c5))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs target loop-extraction performance, --build-config path shortening, and a stack overflow fix; 6.4 release tracking opened.
repositories.activity:
- **Loop extraction from toplevel into helper closures** opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2245](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2245)): moves outermost loops of the entry function into small helper closures so JS/Wasm engines tier-compile only the helpers rather than the large initialization function; supersedes the earlier Wasm-only variant ([#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)) which was closed in its favor
- **`--build-config` output shortening** opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2247](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2247)): compact encoding omits keys at their default values (e.g. `effects=jspi` instead of `effects=jspi+toplevel=false+use-js-string=true`), keeping dune-derived build-config directory names within Windows `MAX_PATH`; discussion ongoing about whether a more cryptic but shorter encoding should be used for all configs
- **Stack overflow fix for `--effects=double-translation`** draft PR opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)): adds a `caml_direct_trampoline` loop for SCC-paired direct-style closures to prevent JS stack exhaustion on deep mutual recursion; includes a regression test at depth 1,000,000
- **js_of_ocaml 6.4 release tracking issue** opened by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241)) ahead of OCaml 5.5, listing candidates: Promise/Fetch bindings ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)), Wasm WASI runtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and small fixes ([#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240))
- **CI fix for dune 3.23.1** merged by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([#2242](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2242)), restoring a broken master build
- **Ongoing blocked PRs**: WASI runtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) and Wasm native effects via Stack Switching ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) remain blocked on [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613) and a new dune release; dune 3.23 migration ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) awaits resolution of [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438)
- **Promise and Fetch bindings** PR ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) received review comments from [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) on `Headers` constructor array format and rejection handling; `headers_of_list` added in response
- **OCaml 5.5 test suite update** (draft, [#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2195)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)) remains open; core 5.5 runtime support already landed on master
repositories.discussions_summary: Stack overflow in browsers under --effects=double-translation traced to CPS mutual recursion in herd7's interpreter, not the menhir parser.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Stack overflow in web browsers** (issue [#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122)): detailed investigation by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) with instrumentation revealed that for the `herd7` AArch64 memory model simulator the overflow originates in the `.cat` interpreter's `Interpreter.run`/`Interpreter.eval` mutual recursion (reaching 1745 JS frames), not in the menhir parser; the recursion hops through CPS continuation arguments that are opaque to jsoo's tail-call flattening, so frames accumulate linearly with AST depth; `--effects=cps` reduces depth to ~149 frames; a dedicated draft fix PR ([#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)) was opened after confirming `--effects=double-translation` itself causes stack overflows when combined with `--pretty`
- **Release 6.4 scope discussion** (issue [#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241)): [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) asks [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) whether the dune 3.23 migration PR ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) should be included; decision to hold it since bumping `lang dune 3.23` changes sandboxing behavior for user rules and a 6.4 release targeted at OCaml 5.5 does not strictly require it
repositories.trends_summary: Development is converging on Wasm correctness fixes, OxCaml compatibility, and a 6.4 release ahead of OCaml 5.5.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Wasm backend correctness** continues to receive focused attention, with multiple compiler-internal fixes this week for exception handler traversal ([#2244](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2244)) and closure type registration ([#2246](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2246)), unblocking the larger WASI ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) and stack-switching ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) PRs
- **Performance through JIT tiering awareness**: the loop extraction PR ([#2245](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2245)) reflects an explicit strategy of structuring output code to work with JS/Wasm engine tiering heuristics rather than relying solely on optimizing the bytecode-to-output translation
- **OxCaml compatibility track** is being actively maintained alongside mainline OCaml support, with PR [#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225) catching up to OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 including SIMD semantics parity
- **Stack overflow mitigation** is an active area: both a user-facing bug ([#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122)) and a compiler-level fix ([#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)) are in progress simultaneously, exploring trampoline-based solutions for `--effects=double-translation`
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon drove Wasm and OxCaml fixes; @hhugo opened release tracking, a stack overflow fix draft, and debugged the herd7 browser issue.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged five PRs this week: Wasm runtime small fixes ([#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240)), CI fix for dune 3.23.1 ([#2242](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2242)), OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update ([#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)), Wasm `needed_handlers` fix ([#2244](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2244)), and Wasm closure env type names fix ([#2246](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2246)); also opened loop extraction PR ([#2245](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2245)) and `--build-config` shortening PR ([#2247](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2247)); provided review comments on Promise/Fetch bindings ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)) and participated in release scope discussion ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened the js_of_ocaml 6.4 release tracking issue ([#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241)), opened a draft PR fixing stack overflow in `--effects=double-translation` mutual recursion ([#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)), and conducted detailed debugging of the herd7 browser stack overflow with instrumentation ([#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122))

repositories.brief_summary: Week 21 saw 31 commits across 361 files with preemptible fibers landing, fields-of-kind-any merged, and many backend and flambda2 improvements.
repositories.new_features_summary: Preemptible fibers, fields and constructor args of kind `any`, support for arrays with unboxed DWARF elements, and persistent modules with file-level staticity modality were all merged.
repositories.new_features:
- **Preemptible fibers** merged via [#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark): adds a `tickc` callback to fibers invoked on each runtime tick, enabling user-controlled preemption without an interval timer.
- **Fields and constructor args of kind `any`** merged via [#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461) by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer): allows declared field/arg types to have kind `any`; use sites require a more refined representable kind.
- **Support for arrays with unboxed elements in DWARF** merged via [#6102](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6102) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon): emits correct per-element byte strides for int8#, int32#, float#, and unboxed products in debug info.
- **Persistent modules with file-level staticity modality** merged via [#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): a persistent module is static iff its `.mli` carries `@@ static` or `@@ dynamic`; a new `cmi_staticity` CMI field propagates this.
- **Simple `open` in quotes** merged via [#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6092) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski): enables `let open` and `M.(...)` syntax inside quoted expressions.
- **Allow referencing current compilation unit in quotes** merged via [#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6086) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski): quoted expressions can now refer to identifiers from the module being compiled.
- **Structured Fexpr primitive** merged via [#5825](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5825): extends the fexpr intermediate language with structured primitive representations.
- **Implement missing fexpr primitives and types** merged via [#6122](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6122) by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev): completes fexpr with recursive type support and missing primitive cases.
repositories.activity_summary: 31 commits merged during the week spanning x86 binary emitter fixes, flambda2 compilation speed improvements, GC correctness fixes, and a range of new open PRs.

- [#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028) merged by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon): fixes NOP encoding in the x86 binary emitter, using proper multi-byte NOPs in text sections.

repositories.discussions_summary: Active review threads on preemptible fibers internals, CFG branch folding correctness, and the DWARF attribute convention change for OxCaml types.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#6104](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6104) CFG jump threading by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) drew review questions about whether the reaching-definition walk could incorrectly bypass terminator call results and whether exceptional predecessors are handled safely.
- [#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) preemptible fibers discussion included debate on stack alignment, the unified `effect_handler` type, and the `noalloc` property of `caml_continuation_update_tick_handler_noexc`.
- [#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6093) DWARF emission rework by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) is a planned breaking change for the OxCaml LLDB plugin, dropping the custom `DW_AT_ocaml_offset_record_from_pointer` attribute in favour of standard DWARF expressions.
- [#6116](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6116) more general file sections received an AI-assisted code review via [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) identifying concerns around the dual builder/field representation in `Compilenv` and dead exports in `Flambda_cmx_format`.
- [#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106) making `[@@flatten_floats]` optional drew feedback from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on whether the attribute should also set `unboxable_default` to false and on error message clarity.
- [#6125](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6125) annotating `(==)` and `(!=)` args as `@ immutable` was closed without merging: [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) noted it would break existing partial applications and suggested waiting for proper mode polymorphism.
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing work focuses on x86 binary emitter correctness and code size, compilation speed via flambda2 optimisations, and expanding the unboxed types and modes systems.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The x86 binary emitter has received sustained attention this week with fixes for NOP encoding, text-section alignment, 16-bit immediate encoding, reg-reg move canonicalization, and the `ud2` guard for data-in-text ([#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029), [#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027), [#6023](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6023), [#6129](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6129)).
- Compilation speed continues to be a theme: Patricia tree efficiency improvements ([#6068](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6068)), in-types depth variable erasure ([#6112](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6112)), and avoiding unnecessary cmx loading ([#6065](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6065)) all target flambda2 compile times.
- The unboxed types system is being refined: `[@@flatten_floats]` is becoming an opt-in escape hatch ([#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106)), float record block indices are being removed ([#6095](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6095)), and atomic field semantics for unboxed records are being defined ([#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124), [#6131](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6131), [#6130](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6130)).
- Zero-alloc region tracking is being built up incrementally: [#6111](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6111) removed `alloc_mode` from `Set_of_closures.t` and [#6121](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6121) adds `alloc_regions` to primitives as groundwork.
- Metaprogramming (runtime eval/quotes) continues to expand with `open` in quotes ([#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6092)), current-unit references in quotes ([#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6086)), and fexpr completions ([#6122](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6122)).

repositories.brief_summary: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.
repositories.new_features_summary: New io_uring ops (send_zc, sendmsg_zc, recv_zc, epoll_wait, pipe, etc.) and Setup_flags (hybrid_iopoll, cqe_mixed, sqe_mixed) added.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **GC registration bug** in `ocaml_uring_setup` where the `polling_timeout` (`int option`) parameter was not registered with the GC via `CAMLparam`, which could cause memory corruption when the GC moves values; reported by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) ([#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)) and fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136))
- Added **`send_zc` and `sendmsg_zc` zero-copy send operations** (`IORING_OP_SEND_ZC`, `IORING_OP_SENDMSG_ZC`) to the capability discovery system in `discover.ml` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139))
- Added **seven further new io_uring operations** to `discover.ml` as part of the liburing 2.14 update: `IORING_OP_RECV_ZC`, `IORING_OP_EPOLL_WAIT`, `IORING_OP_READV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_WRITEV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_PIPE`, `IORING_OP_NOP128`, and `IORING_OP_URING_CMD128` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Added **four new `Setup_flags`** to the OCaml API in `uring.mli`: `no_sqarray` (fail on older indirect submission systems), `hybrid_iopoll` (delayed I/O completion polling to reduce CPU usage), `cqe_mixed` (mixed 16/32-bit responses to save memory), and `sqe_mixed` (mixed 64/128-bit requests to save memory), with documentation pointing to `io_uring_setup_flags(7)` ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **vendored liburing to version 2.14** (from the previous version), incorporating 305 changed files with new operations, examples, tests, and man pages for features like `io_uring_resize_rings`, `io_uring_register_region`, zero-copy receive (`zcrx`), and `io_uring_submit_and_wait_min_timeout` by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138))
- Updated **README.md documentation** with instructions for updating liburing to new versions, covering the `git subtree pull` workflow and required edits to `dune` and `discover.ml` ([#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137))
repositories.activity_summary: All four PRs opened this week were merged: GC bug fix, build docs, liburing 2.14 update, and new zero-copy ops.

repositories.brief_summary: Added missing_ok option to Eio.Path.unlink; draft PR opened for io_uring performance flags.
repositories.new_features_summary: Eio.Path.unlink gains optional ~missing_ok parameter to silently ignore missing files.
repositories.new_features: - **`Eio.Path.unlink`** gains an optional `~missing_ok` parameter: when set to `true`, the function silently does nothing if the target path doesn't exist rather than raising an `Fs.Not_found` IO error ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), resolves [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)). This mirrors the existing `~missing_ok` behaviour in `rmtree` and avoids the boilerplate of catching `Not_found` at call sites.
repositories.activity_summary: Draft PR for io_uring performance flags opened; incremental directory reading PR received design feedback.
repositories.activity:
- **io_uring performance flags** draft PR opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)), proposing `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` and `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` flags for `eio_linux` which showed a large speed improvement on HTTP benchmarks; currently blocked by the benchmark CI machine running Linux 5.15 which is too old to support these flags.
- **Incremental directory reading** draft PR ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) received design input from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), with the discussion favouring a non-recursive `walk` API that hands off descending logic to the caller, enabling future parallel or sequential traversal primitives to be built on top.
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion on incremental directory walk API: non-recursive approach preferred to keep primitives composable.
repositories.notable_discussions: - **Walk API design** for [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) discussed whether `Eio.Path.walk` should recurse automatically or yield a single directory's entries and leave recursion to the user. The agreed direction is the non-recursive form, so a separate parallel or sequential recursion helper can be layered on top without baking the choice into the primitive.
repositories.trends_summary: Incremental improvements to the filesystem API continue, with both ergonomics (missing_ok) and performance (io_uring flags) in focus.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **filesystem API** is receiving incremental ergonomic improvements, with `missing_ok` on `unlink` ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) following the same pattern already present in `rmtree`, suggesting a broader push toward consistent optional-error semantics across path operations.
- **io_uring backend performance** is an active concern, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) exploring newer kernel flags ([#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) working on **incremental directory reading** ([#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) that already shows 10-13x speedups on Linux.
repositories.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch contributed the merged feature; Thomas Leonard reviewed, merged, and opened a new draft; Patrick Ferris and Anil Madhavapeddy advanced the walk API design.
repositories.contributors:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) authored and submitted PR [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) adding `~missing_ok` to `Eio.Path.unlink`, originally proposed in issue [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) reviewed and merged [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), closed issue [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828), and opened the draft PR [#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840) exploring io_uring performance flags for `eio_linux`.
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) continued work on the incremental directory reading draft PR [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821), which received an update this week.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) provided design feedback on [#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821), steering the API toward a non-recursive walk primitive.

repositories.brief_summary: Stéphane Glondu reported and submitted a fix for Task.setup_pool failing hard on domain allocation errors.
repositories.activity_summary: A new PR proposes graceful fallback in Task.setup_pool when domain allocation fails.
repositories.activity: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) proposing that **`Task.setup_pool` gracefully falls back** to fewer domains when domain allocation fails, rather than raising an exception, fixing the issue reported in [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133); the PR touches 3 files with a small change (7 additions, 7 deletions) and is marked mergeable
repositories.discussions_summary: Issue #133 raises a usability concern about Task.setup_pool failing hard on domain allocation errors.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) filed [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) arguing that **`Task.setup_pool`** should not fail entirely when it cannot allocate all requested domains, but instead fall back gracefully to however many domains it could create — a robustness improvement for environments with limited domain availability
repositories.trends_summary: Robustness of pool initialization under resource-constrained environments is an emerging focus.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The pairing of issue [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) and PR [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) reflects interest in making **`Task.setup_pool` more resilient** to runtime constraints, following a pattern of hardening the library for real-world deployment scenarios where the requested number of domains may not always be available
repositories.contributors_summary: Stéphane Glondu opened both the issue and the corresponding fix PR this week.
repositories.contributors: - [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) both identified and addressed the `Task.setup_pool` robustness problem, filing issue [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133) and immediately following up with PR [#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134) to implement a fallback mechanism

group_summaries.brief_summary: TLS/tar security fixes, lrgrep 0.9, dune 3.23.1, Windows CI overhaul, and ocaml.org tutorial improvements.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: TLS 2.1.0 patches two CVEs; lrgrep 0.9 debuts; dune 3.23.1 fixes menhir injection; Emacs Combobulate docs added.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **tls 2.1.0** fixes two security vulnerabilities (`keyUsage`/`extendedKeyUsage` validation missing from server handshakes — CVE-2026-45389/OSEC-2026-07 — and from TLS 1.3 client handshakes — CVE-2026-45388/OSEC-2026-06), reported by Ben Smyth; also adds `drain_handshake` improvements ([ocaml/opam-repository#29930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29930))
- **tar 3.5.0** fixes an integer-overflow security issue from `Int64.to_int` on 32-bit platforms in tar-unix, tar-lwt-unix, and tar-eio; raises OCaml lower bound to 4.13.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29940))
- **lrgrep 0.9** first public release — precise LR coverage enumeration, JSON report output, a "Getting started" guide, and a `lrgrep.top` functor for customising lexical syntax; applied to OCaml with exhaustive coverage ([ocaml/opam-repository#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928))
- **ocaml-compiler 5.4.1+lrgrep** experimental compiler fork by [Frédéric Bour](https://github.com/let-def) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) integrates lrgrep-based improved syntax error messages into OCaml 5.4.1; tagged `avoid-version` so it is only selected when explicitly requested ([ocaml/opam-repository#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932))
- **dune 3.23.1** fixes menhir opam dependency injection, restores 3.22 version-bound deduplication behaviour, fixes secondary compiler fallback for packages with OCaml upper bounds below 4.14, and fixes NetBSD bootstrap ([ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29903)); changelog entry also added to ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3660](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3660))
- **Windows dependency graph overhaul** by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): fixes incorrect `{post}` dependencies in `ocaml-env-*` packages, moves `mingw-w64-shims` into `ocaml-env-mingw`, and introduces `x-revision` fields for tracked in-place updates ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907))
- **valkey 0.4.0** adds typed OCaml wrappers for Valkey Bundle Search (`Valkey.Search`), JSON (`Valkey.Json`), and Bloom filters (`Valkey.Bloom`) with runnable examples ([ocaml/opam-repository#29916](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29916))
- **awa 0.6.0** (SSH in OCaml) adds a server `eof` function and replaces `cstruct.t` with `string`/`Buffer.t` for internal state ([ocaml/opam-repository#29948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29948))
- **symphony-orchestrator-tui 0.1.0** new terminal UI toolkit with buffered renderer, Toffee-backed flexbox layout, reusable widgets, key parsing, and viewport helpers ([ocaml/opam-repository#29910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29910))
- **ecma-regex 0.1.0** new library for ECMAScript regular-expression syntax, Unicode property escapes, raw UTF-16 String APIs, and JSON Schema regex compatibility ([ocaml/opam-repository#29915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29915))
- **Emacs Combobulate docs** added to the editor setup tutorial on ocaml.org explaining tree-sitter-based structured navigation and editing ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **ocaml/opam-repository** processed a high volume of activity: two CVE fixes (tls 2.1.0 via [ocaml/opam-repository#29930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29930), tar 3.5.0 via [ocaml/opam-repository#29940](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29940)), the debut of lrgrep 0.9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928)), dune 3.23.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29903)), a Windows CI dependency graph overhaul ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907)), and over 20 new or updated packages merged
- **ocaml/ocaml.org** received tutorial improvements (Emacs Combobulate docs [ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603), `--with-dev-setup` flag documentation [ocaml/ocaml.org#3646](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3646)), the dune 3.23.1 changelog entry ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3660](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3660)), and four Planet scrape PRs covering OxCaml packaging, the OCaml Weekly News, and MirageOS UI work
- Combined, 26+ contributors were active across the two repositories spanning security, infrastructure, package submissions, documentation, and content curation
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dependency constraint fixes, MirageOS metrics deduplication, Camlp5/OCaml 5.5 updates, and several large open PRs awaiting review dominated ongoing activity.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Open PRs awaiting merge in ocaml/opam-repository**: [awso 0.9.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29921) — large AWS API library for 400+ services by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella), blocked on Windows path-length issues and [ocaml/opam-repository#29939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29939); [slipshow 0.11.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29951) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) featuring a new LSP server with diagnostics, hover docs, and go-to-definition; [z3 4.16.0](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29933) by [@filipeom](https://github.com/filipeom) with active discussion on GCC version constraints for older distros; [caqti 2.3.1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937) by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) (missing `caqti-tls-async` depopt under investigation); [ocaml-option-llvm](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29582) by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) for LLVM/Clang toolchain support (long-running review with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
