- **MirageOS and networking** saw coordinated releases merged by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm): mirage-net-solo5 0.8.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29946)), utcp 0.0.4 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29947)) — both deduplicating metrics sources — and awa 0.6.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29948](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29948)); a constraint relaxation for ocaml-solo5 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29944)) was discussed and closed in favour of handling it via downstream opam repos
- **Camlp5 ecosystem** received coordinated updates by [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy): camlp5 8.05.01 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29913)) with OCaml 5.5 syntax support, pa_ppx 8.05.01 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29920](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29920)), the pa_ppx suite version-number sync fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#29931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29931)), and camlp5-buildscripts 0.07 for bos 0.3.0/OCaml 5.5 coexistence ([ocaml/opam-repository#29950](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29950))
- **Dependency constraint fixes** merged throughout the week: ppxlib upper bounds for rdf_ppx packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29942](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29942) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)); lwt.5.10.0 upper bound on OCaml 5.5 due to `socketaddr.h` API change ([ocaml/opam-repository#29923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29923) by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)); dune-configurator.3.23.0 lower bound raised to OCaml 4.14 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29924](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29924) by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid)); fluxt constrained to `tar < 3.5.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29943](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29943) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm))
- **Other merged packages**: MlFront 2.4.2.168 (21 packages) by [@jonahbeckford](https://github.com/jonahbeckford) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29918)); integers 0.8.0 by [Jeremy Yallop](https://github.com/yallop) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29917)); visitors.20260520 by [François Pottier](https://github.com/fpottier) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29926](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29926)); conf-rocksdb 2 migrated to pkg-config ([ocaml/opam-repository#29919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29919)); ocaml-dockerfile 8.4.0 with `Distro.gcc_version` by [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29927)); irky 0.1 IRC client by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29925)); odep 0.2.2 by [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29935))
- **ocaml.org open PRs**: Tour of OCaml readability improvements by [Anirudh S](https://github.com/anirudh24seven) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3654](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3654)); Hashtbl vs Maps clarification by [@LordPatate](https://github.com/LordPatate) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631)); platform release changelogs consolidation by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) covering dune-release 2.2.1, mdx 2.5.2, ocamlformat 0.29.0, merlin v5.7, ocaml-lsp 1.26.0, OCaml 5.5.0~beta1, utop 2.17.0, odoc 3.2.0, and opam-publish 3.0.0
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune 3.23.1** landed in both repositories simultaneously: the package was merged into [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29903)) and a corresponding changelog entry was added to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3660](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3660)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- **OxCaml ecosystem**: The lrgrep/ocaml-compiler packages landing in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928), [ocaml/opam-repository#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932)) is reflected in the [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) Planet scrapes ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3659), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3661](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3661)), which added posts by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) covering OxCaml packaging for Debian, Fedora, Homebrew, and Arch
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release preparation**: The `lwt.5.10.0` upper bound fix for OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29923)) and camlp5 5.5 compatibility updates ([ocaml/opam-repository#29913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29913)) in opam-repository align with the OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 changelog being drafted in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)
- **avoid-version syntax fix**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) quickly filed [ocaml/opam-repository#29941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29941) to fix a syntax regression introduced by the lrgrep compiler PR ([ocaml/opam-repository#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932)), showing rapid iteration between package submission and opam tooling correctness
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Windows compiler dependency invariant** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907)): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) and [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) debated whether conf packages should use `x-revision` fields or version bumps to track in-place fixes; consensus leaned toward `x-revision` to avoid leaving known-broken old packages accessible to users
- **ocaml-solo5 cross-compiler invariant** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29944)): [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) explained that the current `=` version constraint makes host/cross compiler ABI compatibility easy to reason about; relaxing it without a clear invariant risks silently breaking MirageOS cross-compilation; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) closed the PR in favour of handling it downstream
- **Z3 4.16.0 GCC version handling** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29933)): [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposed adding centos-7, centos-9, debian-12, ubuntu-22.04, opensuse-15.6, and oraclelinux-7 to `x-ci-accept-failures` due to C++20 `<format>` header unavailability on older GCC versions
- **conf-bzip2 versioning policy** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29912)): [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) questioned whether large conf package changes require a version bump; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) argued in-place editing is preferable to leaving permanent bugs in old versions
- **Cram test strategy** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29929](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29929)): [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) advised [Emile Trotignon](https://github.com/EmileTrotignon) that including cram tests in opam releases is fragile since unrelated dependency updates can break them for trivial formatting reasons
- **Wrong dates on news page** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3627](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3627)): Reported by [@756D61](https://github.com/756D61) and fixed by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3653](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3653)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility wave**: Multiple simultaneous updates targeting OCaml 5.5 — camlp5 8.05.01 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29913)), lwt upper bound ([ocaml/opam-repository#29923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29923)), the lrgrep compiler fork ([ocaml/opam-repository#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932)), and OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 changelog in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641) — indicate active ecosystem preparation for the upcoming release
- **Windows CI infrastructure maturation**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) continues systematic overhaul of Windows packaging across consecutive PRs ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29907), [ocaml/opam-repository#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29912)); introduction of `x-revision` fields signals a policy shift toward tracked in-place updates
- **LR-based error message tooling gaining traction**: Simultaneous landing of lrgrep 0.9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29928)) and the `ocaml-compiler.5.4.1+lrgrep` fork ([ocaml/opam-repository#29932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29932)) reflects a coordinated push to bring improved parser error messages to the OCaml ecosystem
- **MirageOS metrics deduplication**: Multiple MirageOS packages (mirage-net-solo5 [ocaml/opam-repository#29946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29946), utcp [ocaml/opam-repository#29947](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29947)) received the same pattern of removing per-instance `Metrics.Src.t` in favour of `ifname`/`stack-id` tags, suggesting a coordinated cleanup
- **Beginner documentation focus on ocaml.org**: Two open PRs — Tour of OCaml readability ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3654](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3654)) and Hashtbl vs Maps explanation ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3631](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3631)) — reflect ongoing community effort to improve onboarding material
- **OxCaml deployment story expanding**: Planet posts this week covered packaging OxCaml for Debian, Fedora, Homebrew, and Arch ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3659](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3659), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3661](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3661)), alongside the lrgrep tooling that underpins the OxCaml fork's improved error messages
- **AI-adjacent OCaml packages increasing**: openrouter_api 0.1.0 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29914)) — a unified LLM gateway client — and the AI-assisted MlFront_ProgressZig component in [ocaml/opam-repository#29918](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29918) reflect growing interest in AI tooling within the OCaml ecosystem

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.24 prep with Melange and OxCaml support; odoc adds kind annotations; opam repository overhaul continues; js_of_ocaml 6.4 tracking opened.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.24: directory targets, Melange preprocess fields; odoc: OxCaml kind annotations; js_of_ocaml: OxCaml SIMD stubs, Wasm fixes; opam: OpamPatch module.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`%{bin:...}` bin-layout support in dune**: [ocaml/dune#14432](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14432) introduces a bin-layout mode for `%{bin:...}` path-form dependencies, allowing executables to be referenced via their installed layout path.
- **Directory targets available in Dune 3.24**: [ocaml/dune#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579) makes directory targets a first-class feature gated on `(lang dune 3.24)`.
- **Melange-specific preprocess fields**: [ocaml/dune#14572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14572) adds `melange.preprocess` and `melange.preprocessor_deps` stanza fields, gated on Dune 3.24, for Melange-only preprocessing configuration.
- **Melange single-context conditional preprocessor deps**: [ocaml/dune#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14681) and [ocaml/dune#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14680) track compilation contexts by mode and add conditional Melange preprocessor dependencies.
- **RPC long-poll cancellation**: [ocaml/dune#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14629) adds the ability to cancel long-poll RPC requests without responding, with supporting tests.
- **Profile name validation**: [ocaml/dune#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14657) validates profile names in the `env` stanza, fixing [ocaml/dune#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14630).
- **OxCaml kind annotations in odoc**: [ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) adds parsing and rendering of OxCaml kind annotations across all output formats (HTML, LaTeX, man, markdown), introducing a new `Kind.t` model type with variants for `Default`, `Abbreviation`, `Mod`, `With`, `Kind_of`, and `Product`.
- **OxCaml implementation file rendering tested in odoc**: [ocaml/odoc#1424](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1424) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) adds test coverage for how `.ml` files without an interface are rendered using a sample from OxCaml documentation.
- **New `OpamPatch` module in opam**: [ocaml/opam#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934) extracts patch operations from `OpamSystem` into a dedicated module providing `translate_patch`, `patch`, and `parse_patch` functions.
- **New `OpamRepositoryRoot` module in opam**: [ocaml/opam#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) abstracts repository root operations (quarantine, backup, move, copy, patch, path) behind a clean interface, with `OpamRepositoryPath` functorized via a new `Make` functor.
- **OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 update in js_of_ocaml**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) adds precise SIMD `float32` min/max bytecode semantics, new stubs for `caml_no_bytecode_impl` and `caml_reinterpret_unboxed_int64_as_tagged_int63`, and `caml_obj_dup` improvements.
- **Wasm closure env type names fix in js_of_ocaml**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2246](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2246) resolves a struct type clash between `no_code_pointer`-optimised closures and arity-0 closures causing `struct.new` inconsistencies.
- **Wasm `needed_handlers` traversal fix in js_of_ocaml**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2244](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2244) corrects exception handler block traversal to walk the dominator subtree rather than CFG-reachable blocks.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Five repositories had significant activity this week: [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) (90+ PRs), [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin), [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc), [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam), and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml); [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) and [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind) had minor activity; [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format) and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded activity.
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) was the highest-volume repository with the 3.23.1 release landing on main and the 3.24.0 release branch opened via [ocaml/dune#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14621).
- OxCaml support is a cross-cutting theme: [ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544) added `oxcaml-compiler` recognition, [ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) added kind annotation rendering, and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225) caught up to OxCaml 5.2.0minus31.
- The [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) repository overhaul ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) advanced with two prerequisite modules merged and three new test and abstraction PRs opened.
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) opened its 6.4 release tracking issue ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241)) ahead of OCaml 5.5, with Wasm backend correctness receiving multiple targeted fixes.
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) saw three maintenance merges and two major feature PRs opened: LRgrep-based syntax error messages ([ocaml/merlin#2072](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2072)) and structural code search via ocamlgrep ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064)).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune scheduler/file-watcher refactoring, opam repository overhaul prerequisites, odoc mdx test removal, and js_of_ocaml Wasm and release prep drove activity.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Dune scheduler and file watcher refactoring**: a series of PRs from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#14669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14669), [#14670](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14670), [#14671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14671), [#14673](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14673), [#14674](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14674), [#14675](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14675), [#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14676)) reduces coupling between the scheduler, file watcher backends, and the build loop; Windows watcher sync fix merged in [ocaml/dune#14678](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14678).
- **Dune 3.24.0 release branch opened**: [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened [ocaml/dune#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14621) with the 3.24.0 release tracker at [ocaml/dune#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14620); `oxcaml-compiler` recognition ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544)) flagged for inclusion before cut.
- **Dune `dune show targets` design revision**: [ocaml/dune#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14665) was merged then reverted via [ocaml/dune#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14710) after design reconsideration; related draft PRs [#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14666) and [#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14667) were closed pending further discussion on [ocaml/dune#8167](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/8167).
- **Dune CI migration**: [ocaml/dune#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14538) switches revdeps CI from Nix-based to opam-based tests, resolving [ocaml/dune#13897](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13897).
- **Merlin maintenance merges**: PPX cache invalidation hash fix ([ocaml/merlin#2070](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2070)), Menhir version update in docs and Nix setup ([ocaml/merlin#2068](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2068)), and OPAM post-install message cleanup ([ocaml/merlin#2069](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2069)).
- **Odoc mdx test infrastructure removed**: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) deleted ~10,500 lines of mdx tests from `src/xref2` and removed mdx from CI via [ocaml/odoc#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437), following agreement that cram tests provide better coverage.
- **Opam repository reftests**: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) opened HTTP repository reftests ([ocaml/opam#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939)) and a comprehensive repository layout reftest ([ocaml/opam#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941)) to lock in current behaviour before the tar.gz-based rewrite lands.
- **Opam `tar` library integration**: [ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) replaces the `tar` system tool dependency with a pure OCaml library for archive creation.
- **Opam `opam config subst` absolute path fix**: [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) opened [ocaml/opam#6936](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6936) resolving [ocaml/opam#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925).
- **Opam `.install` file `prefix`/`prefixexec` sections**: [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) opened [ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) addressing feature request [ocaml/opam#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919).
- **js_of_ocaml 6.4 release tracking opened**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241) listing Promise/Fetch bindings ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)), Wasm WASI runtime ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), and runtime fixes as 6.4 candidates ahead of OCaml 5.5.
- **js_of_ocaml CI fix for dune 3.23.1**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2242](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2242) restoring a broken master build triggered by the new dune release.
- **js_of_ocaml loop extraction performance**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2245](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2245) moving outermost loops into helper closures so JS/Wasm engines tier-compile only the helpers rather than the large initialization function.
- **ocaml-lsp Nix flake update**: the automated PR [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) bumped `merlin5_3` to commit `77009b69` (2026-05-22) and `nixpkgs` to the 2026-05-23 snapshot.
- **ocamlfind `.cmx` installation issue**: [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) opened [ocaml/ocamlfind#117](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/117) proposing that Findlib install `.cmx` files alongside `.cmi` files to resolve OCaml warning 58 (`no-cmx-file`) in downstream projects including Rocq.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OxCaml support coordinated across three repos**: [ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544) added `oxcaml-compiler` package recognition; [ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) merged OxCaml kind annotation rendering with modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and zero alloc annotations ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) in review; [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225) updated to OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 including SIMD semantics parity.
- **Dune 3.23.1 release triggered a js_of_ocaml CI fix**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2242](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2242) was needed immediately after the dune 3.23.1 release to restore the js_of_ocaml master build.
- **js_of_ocaml WASI and stack-switching PRs blocked on dune**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) (Wasm WASI runtime) and [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189) (Wasm native effects) remain blocked on [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613); the dune 3.23 migration ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) awaits [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438).
- **Merlin commit tracked in ocaml-lsp Nix flake**: [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) bumped `merlin5_3` to the [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) commit `77009b69` from 2026-05-22, showing the close dependency between the two repos.
- **[Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai) contributed across dune and ocamlfind**: merged profile name validation in dune ([ocaml/dune#14657](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14657)) and opened the `.cmx` installation issue in ocamlfind ([ocaml/ocamlfind#117](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/117)) in the same week.
- **[Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) active across merlin and odoc**: reviewed the locate fix for let punnings in merlin ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2066)) and provided detailed code review on zero alloc annotation propagation and kind annotations in odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)).
- **[Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) (voodoos) active across merlin and ocaml-lsp**: provided dependency hygiene review on ocamlgrep in merlin ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064)) and proposed a vendored `lev` patch strategy for the FreeBSD portability issue in ocaml-lsp ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Dune `dune show targets` design reversal**: [ocaml/dune#14665](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14665) was merged and then reverted via [ocaml/dune#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14710) after [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) reconsidered the design; related PRs [#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14666) and [#14667](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14667) were closed pending further design discussion referencing [ocaml/dune#8167](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/8167).
- **Dune per-module library dependency narrowing**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb)'s 9-layer PR stack ([ocaml/dune#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14492)) saw active review from [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) and [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) on whether Melange compilation contexts could also benefit from the narrowing.
- **Dune tool management design**: [ocaml/dune#13457](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/13457) received questions from [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) about multi-version tooling UX and alignment with planned auto-locking work.
- **Merlin ocamlgrep dependency hygiene**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) flagged that depending on `base` is not acceptable for Merlin in [ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2064) and suggested reusing `Cmt_cache` and `Locate` module internals; [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) replaced `ppx_sexp_conv` with a hand-rolled `csexp`-based implementation.
- **Merlin locate vs. occurrences trade-off for let punnings**: in [ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2066), [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted the fix improves `locate` but makes `occurrences` behaviour less intuitive; acknowledged as an acceptable trade-off pending a more robust fix.
- **Odoc zero alloc annotation propagation**: [ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) generated discussion between [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) on correctly propagating zero_alloc info through component types; [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) noted that without adding `extr_attrs` to `Value.t` in `xref2/components.ml`, the attribute would be silently dropped in wrapped libraries.
- **Opam patch application failure**: [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) narrowed [ocaml/opam#6937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6937) to the OCaml `opatch` library mishandling patches where a file is modified and then copied, and filed an upstream report at hannesm/patch#43.
- **Opam lock correctness edge cases**: [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) filed two distinct locking bugs in the same week — undefined boolean filter variables becoming fatal under `--locked` ([ocaml/opam#6946](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6946)) and a constraint incorrectly reported as unsatisfied despite the package being installed ([ocaml/opam#6944](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6944)).
- **ocaml-lsp FreeBSD build failure**: [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) and [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) continued discussing [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605), tracing the failure to the vendored `lev` library hard-coding `<sys/statfs.h>`; [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) proposed patching the vendored copy directly as a short-term fix.
- **js_of_ocaml stack overflow in herd7**: detailed debugging in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2122](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2122) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) identified the overflow in `Interpreter.run`/`Interpreter.eval` mutual recursion (reaching 1745 JS frames), leading to a dedicated trampoline-based draft fix PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)).
- **js_of_ocaml 6.4 release scope**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241) discussion decided to hold the dune 3.23 migration ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) out of 6.4 since bumping `lang dune 3.23` changes sandboxing behavior for user rules.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml compatibility as a cross-ecosystem priority**: OxCaml-specific work appeared in dune ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14544)), odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410), [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), and js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)) in the same week, indicating coordinated effort to support OxCaml across the tools stack.
- **Dune scheduler and file watcher decoupling**: a sustained series of refactoring PRs from [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#14669](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14669)–[#14676](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14676)) systematically reduces coupling between build loop components, with several follow-on correctness PRs opened in the same week ([ocaml/dune#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14694), [#14702](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14702), [#14705](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14705)).
- **Melange single-context mode advancing in dune**: [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) merged multiple PRs this week ([ocaml/dune#14572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14572), [#14680](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14680), [#14681](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14681), [#14682](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14682)) building infrastructure for the `single-context-universal` label, with installation support in draft ([ocaml/dune#14672](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14672)).
- **Opam repository overhaul via incremental API abstraction**: the overhaul ([ocaml/opam#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)) advances through a queued-PR strategy — `OpamPatch` ([ocaml/opam#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934)) and `OpamRepositoryRoot` ([ocaml/opam#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)) merged this week; `OpamTar` ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)) immediately queued next — with parallel test coverage being built via reftests.
- **Test infrastructure modernization**: odoc removed its legacy mdx test suite ([ocaml/odoc#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1437)) in favour of cram tests; opam is adding reftests ([ocaml/opam#6939](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6939), [#6941](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6941)) to lock in current repository-handling behaviour before major rewrites.
- **Richer error message infrastructure in merlin**: the LRgrep PR ([ocaml/merlin#2072](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/2072)) introduces a pattern-based approach to specifying syntax error messages with built-in coverage tooling, signalling a longer-term effort to replace ad-hoc error recovery logic.
- **Wasm backend maturity in js_of_ocaml**: multiple compiler-internal correctness fixes landed this week ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2244](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2244), [#2246](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2246)) alongside a stack overflow trampoline fix draft ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2243](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2243)) and a JIT-tiering-aware loop extraction PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2245](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2245)), reflecting sustained focus on Wasm backend quality ahead of the 6.4 release.

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml saw 31 commits with preemptible fibers, fields-of-kind-any, and x86 emitter fixes; opam-repository added dune packages.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Preemptible fibers, fields/constructor args of kind `any`, unboxed DWARF array support, and persistent module staticity modality merged in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Preemptible fibers** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark): adds a `tickc` callback invoked on each runtime tick, enabling user-controlled preemption without an interval timer.
- **Fields and constructor args of kind `any`** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461) by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer): allows declared field/arg types to have kind `any`; use sites require a more refined representable kind.
- **Support for arrays with unboxed elements in DWARF** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6102](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6102) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon): emits correct per-element byte strides for int8#, int32#, float#, and unboxed products in debug info.
- **Persistent modules with file-level staticity modality** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6041](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6041) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn): a persistent module is static iff its `.mli` carries `@@ static` or `@@ dynamic`; a new `cmi_staticity` CMI field propagates this.
- **Simple `open` in quotes** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6092) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski): enables `let open` and `M.(...)` syntax inside quoted expressions.
- **Allow referencing current compilation unit in quotes** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6086) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski): quoted expressions can now refer to identifiers from the module being compiled.
- **Structured Fexpr primitive** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5825](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5825): extends the fexpr intermediate language with structured primitive representations.
- **Implement missing fexpr primitives and types** merged via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6122](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6122) by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev): completes fexpr with recursive type support and missing primitive cases.
- **`unboxed` dependency for `core_extended`** added via [oxcaml/opam-repository#50](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/50) by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): ensures correct opam resolution for projects using unboxed types.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), with 31 commits across 361 files spanning new language features, GC correctness fixes, x86 binary emitter improvements, and flambda2 compile-time optimisations.
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) saw three PRs: one merged dependency fix ([oxcaml/opam-repository#50](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/50)), one large open PR adding dune-built packages ([oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49)), and a user-reported build breakage ([oxcaml/opam-repository#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48)).
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) has one large draft PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) syncing upstream minus-37 changes, currently blocked by merge conflicts.
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week.
- 17 contributors were active across the group, with [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp), [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), and [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) among the most active in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.activity_summary: GC correctness fixes, flambda2 compile-time improvements, x86 emitter canonicalization, and opam package metadata updates drove activity beyond new features.
group_summaries.activity:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6109](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6109) merged by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes): makes `caml_darken` do `cmpxchg` on lazy/forcing header words to fix a GC race; followed by [oxcaml/oxcaml#6120](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6120) reducing its subtlety.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6089](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6089) merged by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes): disables automatic GC compaction when the heap has only one chunk, preventing pathological compaction on every major GC cycle.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6112](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6112) merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp): erases all in-types depth variables in flambda2, yielding a 14% compile-time improvement on `typing/mode.ml`.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6068](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6068) merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): Patricia tree efficiency improvements reducing code size of union-family functions.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6110](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6110) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5543](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5543) merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp): Patricia tree output type sharing and high-bit flags in `Int_ids` for better sub-tree separation.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6126](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6126) merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp): inserts function and value slot projections in deterministic order to avoid hash-dependent output.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6023](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6023) merged by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon): x86 binary emitter fixes for NOP encoding, text-section alignment, 16-bit immediate arithmetic, and reg-reg move canonicalization.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6133](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6133) merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp): reverts [oxcaml/oxcaml#5771](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5771) array element kind meet fix due to crashes in internal optimised builds; [oxcaml/oxcaml#6134](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6134) opened with the corrected approach.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6111](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6111) merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): removes `alloc_mode` from `Set_of_closures.t`, retaining it only for non-toplevel closures.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6100](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6100) merged by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell): re-introduces removal of `imported_names` from Flambda2, reverted after a bug was fixed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6099).
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6095](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6095) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#6035](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6035) merged by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa): removes block indices to float records and refactors record representation recomputation in preparation for the `box_` type operator.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6080](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6080) merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): defines `%obj_xxx` primitives consistently with array primitives under `--disable-flat-float-array`.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5885](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5885) merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp): refactors flambda2 `Meet_env` to centralize name-meet logic.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6107](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6107) merged by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski): bumps the magic number for `5.2.0minus-39`.
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49) opened by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam): adds the remaining dune-built packages (48 files, 1528 lines of opam metadata); open and passing CI.
- [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) updated by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js): large draft sync of upstream minus-37 changes (292,247 additions, 47,677 deletions across 840 files), currently blocked by merge conflicts.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6136](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6136) opened by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) updates the Merlin integration in oxcaml/oxcaml to the `5.2.0minus-39` tag, directly linking compiler development in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) with the fork tracked in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin).
- The magic number bump in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6107](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6107) for `5.2.0minus-39` corresponds to the upstream sync targeted by the draft [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239), reflecting coordinated versioning between the compiler and editor tooling forks.
- The `unboxed` dep fix in [oxcaml/opam-repository#50](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/50) reflects the accumulation of OxCaml-specific type system features (unboxed types from [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)) that require corresponding updates to opam metadata in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository).
- The `ocaml-variants` build breakage reported in [oxcaml/opam-repository#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) was traced to [ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544), with a fix expected in Dune 3.24, illustrating cross-project dependency between oxcaml opam metadata and the upstream Dune package manager.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6104](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6104) CFG jump threading by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) drew review questions about whether the reaching-definition walk could incorrectly bypass terminator call results and whether exceptional predecessors are handled safely.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) preemptible fibers discussion included debate on stack alignment, the unified `effect_handler` type, and the `noalloc` property of `caml_continuation_update_tick_handler_noexc`.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6093) DWARF emission rework by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) is a planned breaking change for the OxCaml LLDB plugin, dropping the custom `DW_AT_ocaml_offset_record_from_pointer` attribute in favour of standard DWARF expressions.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6116](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6116) more general file sections by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) received a code review from [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) identifying concerns around the dual builder/field representation in `Compilenv` and dead exports in `Flambda_cmx_format`.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106) making `[@@flatten_floats]` optional drew feedback from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) on whether the attribute should also set `unboxable_default` to false and on error message clarity.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6125](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6125) annotating `(==)` and `(!=)` args as `@ immutable` was closed without merging: [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) noted it would break existing partial applications and suggested waiting for proper mode polymorphism.
- [oxcaml/opam-repository#48](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/48) reported by [Christian Persson](https://github.com/saser): `ocaml-variants` fails to build in Dune-managed OxCaml projects; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) identified the root cause in [ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544), expected in Dune 3.24.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- The x86 binary emitter received sustained attention with fixes for NOP encoding, text-section alignment, 16-bit immediate encoding, reg-reg move canonicalization, and `ud2` guards for data-in-text ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6028](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6028), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6029](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6029), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6027](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6027), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6023](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6023), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6129](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6129)).
- Flambda2 compile-time performance continues to be a focus: depth variable erasure ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6112](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6112)), Patricia tree efficiency ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6068](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6068), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5543](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5543)), and output type sharing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6110](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6110)) collectively target compile times.
- The unboxed types system is being refined: `[@@flatten_floats]` moving to opt-in ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6106](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6106)), float record block index removal ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6095](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6095)), and atomic field semantics for unboxed records ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6124](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6124), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6131](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6131), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6130](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6130)).
- Zero-alloc region tracking is being built incrementally: `alloc_mode` removed from `Set_of_closures.t` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6111](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6111)) and `alloc_regions` added to primitives as groundwork ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6121](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6121)).
- Metaprogramming (runtime eval/quotes) continues to expand: `open` in quotes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6092)), current-unit references in quotes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6086)), and fexpr completions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6122](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6122)).
- Expansion of dune-ecosystem package coverage in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) continues with [oxcaml/opam-repository#49](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/49) targeting the full set of dune-built packages, while dependency metadata for OxCaml-specific package variants accumulates ([oxcaml/opam-repository#50](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/50)).

group_summaries.brief_summary: Typing recovery mechanism lands in ocaml/ocaml, with Seq.ints_in_range, GC correctness work, and runtime fixes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Typing recovery (-typing-recovery flag), Seq.ints_in_range stdlib addition, and bigarray SUBARRAY crash fix.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Typing recovery** ([ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)): Merlin's typing recovery mechanism upstreamed, enabled via `-typing-recovery`; the typechecker continues past the first error and collects all errors in a file, improving editor integration
- **`-typing-recovery` compiler flag** ([ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)): New CLI flag wired through `ocamlc` and `ocamlopt` to enable multi-error typing mode
- **`Seq.ints_in_range`** ([ocaml/ocaml#10480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10480)): New `Seq.ints_in_range ~first ~last` function added to stdlib, producing a bounded integer sequence (inclusive on both ends); annotated `@since 5.6`
- **Fix for `caml_ba_alloc` SUBARRAY crash** ([ocaml/ocaml#14820](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14820)): Backward-compat fix so old C bindings passing `CAML_BA_SUBARRAY` with `data = NULL` no longer crash; cherry-picked to the 5.5 branch ([ocaml/ocaml#14825](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14825))
- **Nicer PDF manual fonts** ([ocaml/ocaml#14819](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14819)): PDF manual now uses Utopia and Cabin fonts for chapter and section titles
- **Documentation improvements**: `Format` module updated with a working example ([ocaml/ocaml#14826](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14826)); `ocamlrun` manpage synchronised with the manual for `OCAMLRUNPARAM` options ([ocaml/ocaml#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14817)); stale option removed from manpage ([ocaml/ocaml#14815](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14815))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no recorded activity
- 15 PRs merged in ocaml/ocaml spanning the typechecker, stdlib, runtime, build system, and documentation
- 24 open PRs active, including a new GC-pacing logic proposal and several ephemeron correctness fixes
- 13 contributors merged work, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) leading on typechecker fixes and documentation
- Headline landing: typing recovery ([ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)), the largest PR of the week, upstreaming infrastructure previously maintained in Merlin
group_summaries.activity_summary: 15 PRs merged in ocaml/ocaml covering typechecker fixes, runtime correctness, stdlib, and docs; several significant PRs newly opened.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14821](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14821) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole): disable "cold" tests in GitHub Actions CI to speed up the mandatory CI loop
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron): follow-up to typing recovery — freeze paths in the modular-explicits interaction
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14822](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14822) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): extended testsuite for `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing edge cases
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14818](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14818) by [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok): add a testsuite test that fragments sizeclass pools
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14814) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): add `caml_result_find_exception` to `mlvalues.h`
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14812](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14812) by [@agrn](https://github.com/agrn): `comballoc` — emit a single add after a combined allocation
- **Newly opened** [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): ensure no orphaned ephemerons remain across GC cycles
- **Typechecker**: [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241) merged — large typing recovery infrastructure (new `typing_recovery.ml`, `typing_recovery_state.ml`, ~80 new test cases); [ocaml/ocaml#14776](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14776) and [ocaml/ocaml#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14789) fixed `moregen`/GADT and scope-escape corner cases; [ocaml/ocaml#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754) fixed cyclic types from `with type` module constraints; [ocaml/ocaml#14051](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14051) improved error messages for first-class modules with missing constrained types
- **Runtime**: [ocaml/ocaml#14816](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14816) fixed a data race when marking lazy objects using `cmpxchg`; [ocaml/ocaml#14699](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14699) removed unused `caml_stat_alloc_aligned[_noexc]` functions
- **Build and docs**: [ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) minor build fixes for cross-compilation; [ocaml/ocaml#14759](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14759) exposed `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing functions for testing
- **Still open / in progress**: new GC-pacing logic [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796) by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez); ephemeron orphaning cluster ([ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745)) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche); extensible IO channels RFC implementation [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube); constructor eta-expansion proposal [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The extensible IO channels implementation [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) is the compiler-side implementation of an RFC tracked in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), though the RFC repository itself had no recorded activity this week
- The typing recovery feature [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241) originated as infrastructure in the Merlin project and is now upstream in ocaml/ocaml, reducing the divergence between the compiler and editor tooling
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Ephemeron correctness cluster**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has multiple open PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774), [ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813)) addressing ephemeron orphaning across GC cycles, interacting with the major GC ephemeron cleanup from ocaml/ocaml#13629
- **New GC-pacing logic** [ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796): [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) opened a substantial PR implementing a new GC-pacing algorithm, listed as the last remaining item for issue [ocaml/ocaml#14324](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14324); still has open test failures under investigation
- **Typing recovery follow-ups**: immediately after landing [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824) to address a remaining interaction with modular-explicits path freezing
- **Constructor eta-expansion** [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803): [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch) proposed automatically eta-expanding argument-less constructors in function position (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`) without new syntax — marked as preliminary and for discussion
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Merlin-to-compiler upstreaming**: the landing of typing recovery ([ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)) continues absorbing Merlin's compiler patches upstream, reducing maintenance divergence for the Merlin team
- **GC correctness and pacing**: significant attention on ephemeron correctness ([ocaml/ocaml#14813](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14813), [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734)) and a new GC-pacing proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) reflect active work to improve runtime reliability and predictability for multicore OCaml
- **Stdlib surface area expanding**: `Seq.ints_in_range` ([ocaml/ocaml#10480](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10480)) joins recent additions like `List.append_map`, `Array.fold_{left,right}2`, and `Fun.todo` — steady incremental growth of the standard library toward OCaml 5.6
- **Documentation quality push**: PDF manual font improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14819](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14819)), manpage synchronization ([ocaml/ocaml#14817](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14817)), and `Format` documentation updates ([ocaml/ocaml#14826](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14826)) reflect ongoing effort to improve user-facing documentation

group_summaries.brief_summary: ocaml-uring updated to liburing 2.14 with new zero-copy ops and GC fix; eio and domainslib received robustness improvements.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Eio.Path.unlink gains ~missing_ok; ocaml-uring gains zero-copy send/recv ops and new Setup_flags from liburing 2.14.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`Eio.Path.unlink ~missing_ok`**: [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) added an optional `~missing_ok` parameter that silently ignores missing files instead of raising `Fs.Not_found`, mirroring the existing behaviour in `rmtree` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), resolves [ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)).
- **Zero-copy send/recv operations** added to `ocaml-uring`: `IORING_OP_SEND_ZC`, `IORING_OP_SENDMSG_ZC`, and `IORING_OP_RECV_ZC` added to capability discovery by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138)).
- **New io_uring operations** exposed in `ocaml-uring`: `IORING_OP_EPOLL_WAIT`, `IORING_OP_READV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_WRITEV_FIXED`, `IORING_OP_PIPE`, `IORING_OP_NOP128`, and `IORING_OP_URING_CMD128` added as part of the liburing 2.14 update ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138)).
- **New `Setup_flags`** in `ocaml-uring`: `hybrid_iopoll` (delayed I/O completion polling), `cqe_mixed` (mixed 16/32-bit responses), `sqe_mixed` (mixed 64/128-bit requests), and `no_sqarray` added to `uring.mli` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138)).
- **GC safety fix** in `ocaml-uring`: the `polling_timeout` (`int option`) parameter in `ocaml_uring_setup` was missing from `CAMLparam` registration; fixed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) after [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) reported the bug ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136), closes [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in three repositories: [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), and [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib); the remaining six repositories had no activity.
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) saw the highest volume: four PRs opened and merged, including a liburing 2.14 vendor update, a GC safety fix, build documentation improvements, and new zero-copy operation entries.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) merged one user-facing feature ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and has two draft PRs in progress ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840), [ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)).
- [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib) received a community-contributed fix for `Task.setup_pool` error handling ([ocaml-multicore/domainslib#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134)).
- Contributors this week: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog), [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic), [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), and [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu).
group_summaries.activity_summary: liburing 2.14 vendored into ocaml-uring with same-week API surface updates; domainslib Task.setup_pool robustness fix proposed.
group_summaries.activity:
- **liburing 2.14 vendor update** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) in a large squashed subtree merge incorporating 305 changed files, immediately followed by OCaml API additions for new operations and flags ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138)).
- **Build process documentation** for `ocaml-uring` merged, describing the `git subtree pull` workflow for future liburing updates and noting the previously broken subtree scheme ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#137](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/137)).
- **`Task.setup_pool` graceful fallback** PR opened by [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu): rather than raising an exception when domain allocation fails, the pool falls back to fewer domains; the change touches 3 files with 7 additions and 7 deletions ([ocaml-multicore/domainslib#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134), addresses [ocaml-multicore/domainslib#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133)).
- **io_uring performance flags** draft PR opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) proposing `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` and `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` for `eio_linux`; currently blocked because the benchmark CI machine runs Linux 5.15, which is too old to support these flags ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)).
- **Incremental directory reading** draft PR by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) updated and received design feedback; the implementation already shows 10–13x speedups on Linux ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5)'s draft PR exploring `IORING_SETUP_DEFER_TASKRUN` and `IORING_SETUP_SINGLE_ISSUER` in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)) depends directly on the new `Setup_flags` added to [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) this week ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138)); the liburing 2.14 update is a prerequisite for the eio performance work.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) contributed to both [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) (design feedback on [ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) (zero-copy op additions in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139)) in the same week.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Walk API design** in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#821](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/821)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) and [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) settled on a non-recursive form for `Eio.Path.walk` that yields a single directory's entries and leaves recursion to the caller, enabling parallel or sequential traversal primitives to be layered on top without constraining the primitive itself.
- **GC safety in C bindings** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#135](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/135)): [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic) identified that `ocaml_uring_setup` registered only `entries` with `CAMLparam1` but omitted `polling_timeout` (`int option`), leaving it unprotected under GC pressure; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) issued a fix PR within the hour, closed the same day ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#136](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/136)).
- **`Task.setup_pool` failure semantics** in [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib) ([ocaml-multicore/domainslib#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/133)): [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) argued that hard failure on domain allocation is too strict for resource-constrained environments and proposed fallback to a smaller pool as the correct default behaviour.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Rapid liburing tracking**: the pattern of vendoring a new liburing release and immediately updating the OCaml API surface — adding new operation enums, `Setup_flags`, and capability discovery entries in the same week — is evident in the coordinated [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139) PRs.
- **Zero-copy networking path**: `send_zc`, `sendmsg_zc`, and `recv_zc` operations are now exposed in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#138](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/138), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#139](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/139)), and the `eio_linux` backend is simultaneously exploring performance flags ([ocaml-multicore/eio#840](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/840)) — indicating a coordinated push toward higher-throughput I/O in eio.
- **Incremental filesystem API ergonomics**: `~missing_ok` on `Eio.Path.unlink` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) follows the same pattern already established in `rmtree`, suggesting a broader effort to apply consistent optional-error semantics across path operations.
- **Resilience of concurrency primitives**: both the `Task.setup_pool` fallback proposal ([ocaml-multicore/domainslib#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib/issues/134)) and the `~missing_ok` addition ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) reflect a pattern of hardening library interfaces for real-world deployment environments where resource availability cannot be assumed.
